title: Add retry logic with exponential backoff to auth HTTP client
body:
## Background

From PR #268 code review finding.

## Problem

The auth HTTP client has a hardcoded 30-second timeout with no retry logic. A malicious or overloaded auth-service can cause the device flow to fail by:
- Responding slowly (29 seconds each time) during polling
- Transient 5xx errors or network issues

This causes user frustration and makes authentication less resilient.

## Proposed Solution

Implement exponential backoff retry for transient failures:
- Retry on: 5xx status codes, network timeouts
- Max retries: 3
- Delays: 1s, 2s, 4s (exponential)
- Don't retry on: 4xx errors (client errors), successful responses

```go
type retryConfig struct {
    MaxRetries  int
    InitialWait time.Duration
    MaxWait     time.Duration
    Retryable   func(resp *http.Response, err error) bool
}
```

## Impact

More resilient authentication flow, better UX during temporary service issues.

## References

- PR #268
- Finding fingerprint: `39b2aa8b9cb64dd1f282d45bf6ce7dde`
